narrative:
First Warn Forecast
Saturday, May 30, 2026

Clouds, showers, thunderstorms, and pockets of fog will continue to impact portions of Missouri and Illinois through the weekend and into Monday before a quieter and drier pattern settles in next week. While not everyone will see rain all day, several rounds of scattered showers and storms are expected, especially during the afternoon and overnight hours.

Early this morning, areas of dense fog and low clouds developed across parts of central and southern Missouri with some visibility dropping below one-quarter mile at times. Motorists traveling early today should be prepared for rapidly changing visibility, especially on rural roads and low-lying areas.

As we move through the day, a nearly stationary frontal boundary stretched from the Mid-South into the Central Plains will continue acting as the focus for scattered showers and thunderstorms across the region. The atmosphere remains humid and unstable enough to support locally heavy rainfall along with a few stronger storms capable of producing gusty winds and small hail.

Rain coverage will remain scattered today, meaning not everyone gets wet at the same time, but keep the umbrella nearby if you have outdoor plans. Any storm that develops will be capable of producing brief downpours due to the high moisture content in the atmosphere.

Storm activity should diminish somewhat this evening before another round of thunderstorms develops to our west over Kansas and western Missouri. Some of those storms may drift into central and northeast Missouri late tonight into early Sunday morning.

Sunday will feature another chance for scattered showers and thunderstorms, mainly during the morning and afternoon hours. A few storms could become locally strong with heavy rainfall and gusty winds possible once again.

Looking ahead into Sunday night and Monday, another disturbance moving southeast through the Midwest may bring additional rounds of showers and thunderstorms. Some stronger storms cannot be ruled out overnight Sunday into early Monday with isolated heavy rainfall remaining the primary concern.

The good news? Drier and more comfortable weather is expected to gradually build into the region beginning Tuesday and continuing through much of next week as cooler north-to-northeast flow settles in behind the departing storm system.

Temperatures through the forecast period will remain seasonable with highs generally in the upper 70s to middle 80s and overnight lows mainly in the 60s.

Regional Breakdown
St. Louis Metro

Scattered showers and thunderstorms today through Monday with dry periods mixed in between. Watch for locally heavy rain and occasional lightning. Highs near 82.

Central Missouri

The greatest chance for repeated rounds of rain and storms will occur across central Missouri through Sunday night. Locally heavy rainfall and brief gusty winds are possible. Highs in the lower 80s.

Southeast Missouri

Patchy dense fog early today followed by scattered afternoon showers and storms. Humid conditions continue through Monday before drier weather arrives. Highs around 80 to 83.

Southern Illinois

A mix of clouds, humidity, and scattered storms through the weekend. Some storms may produce heavy rainfall and brief reduced visibility. Highs in the lower 80s.

Keep the rain gear handy through Monday as several rounds of showers and thunderstorms continue across the region. While widespread severe weather is not expected, locally heavy rainfall, lightning, and a few gusty storms remain possible. Drier weather finally arrives by the middle of next week.
